steps.title: Use the genetic evidence
steps.content: The two condors hatched from eggs laid by females in the enclosure, but their DNA was not similar enough to any male there for those males to be their fathers.

steps.title: Reject unsupported reproduction claims
steps.content:
• Choice A: The evidence concerns the two individuals' origins, not their mothers' origins.
  • Choice B: The passage gives no information about wild California condors.
  • Choice D: The enclosure had males, so the passage does not show that lack of male access triggers parthenogenesis.

steps.title: Infer all-maternal reproduction
steps.content: Since parthenogenesis produces offspring from a mother without mating, the DNA pattern supports the conclusion that the mothers likely reproduced that way. The answer is C.
